Home / Runway Calculator

Runway Calculator

Calculate how many months your startup can survive at current burn rate. Compare scenarios by adjusting burn rates to extend your runway.

How to Use This Tool

1
Enter your total cash on hand (bank balance + accessible funds).
2
Enter your total monthly burn rate (all operating expenses).
3
Enter your current monthly revenue (if any).
4
Review your runway in months and the exact date your cash runs out.
5
Check the burn multiple to see how efficiently you are using cash.
6
Scroll down to see 5 comparison scenarios with adjusted burn rates.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is a good runway for a startup?

Most VCs recommend 18-24 months of runway after each funding round. This gives you enough time to hit milestones for the next round without being under immediate pressure. For bootstrapped solopreneurs, 6-12 months is a reasonable minimum — but more is always better.

What is a burn multiple and why does it matter?

Burn multiple = net burn / net new ARR. A burn multiple under 1.0x is excellent, 1.0-1.5x is good, and above 2.0x is concerning. It measures how efficiently you turn cash into growth. Investors scrutinize this metric heavily.

How can I extend my runway without raising money?

Three ways: (1) Cut costs — audit every subscription and tool, negotiate with vendors. (2) Increase revenue — raise prices, upsell existing customers, launch a new pricing tier. (3) Improve cash collection — switch to annual billing, shorten payment terms, offer discounts for upfront payments.

Should I include my own salary in the burn rate?

Yes. Your burn rate should include ALL operating expenses: salaries (including your own), rent, SaaS tools, marketing, legal, and a 10-15% buffer for unexpected costs. Underestimating burn is one of the most common reasons startups run out of cash.

What if my revenue covers my burn?

If you are cash-flow positive, congratulations! You have effectively infinite runway. At this stage, reinvest profits into growth to compound faster. Consider hiring, increasing marketing spend, or building a cash buffer for strategic opportunities.

Results

Copy All
#1 🚀 Runway Analysis • Cash on Hand: $50,000 • Monthly Burn: $15,000 • Monthly Revenue: $3,000 • Net Monthly Burn: $12,000 • Burn Multiple: 4.00x ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ • Months of Runway: 4.2 months • Cash Run-out Date: October 15, 2026 🟡 WARNING: 3-6 months of runway. Start fundraising conversations now. 💡 Tip: Aim for 18-24 months of runway after each fundraise. This gives you enough time to hit the next milestone before needing to raise again.
#2 Reduce burn 10%: Burn $13,500/mo → 5.2 months of runway
#3 Reduce burn 25%: Burn $11,250/mo → 7.1 months of runway
#4 Reduce burn 50%: Burn $7,500/mo → 16.7 months of runway
#5 Increase burn 10%: Burn $16,500/mo → 3.5 months of runway
